description: Backlog (live tail entries + object count + snapshot-over-ceiling) vs write rate, per maintenance trigger (in-band write-tick / scheduled cron) and profile (cf-free / node). Measures whether FREE-RATE maintenance keeps up within the ~30 writes/min M-size envelope. Measures only.

methodology.simulated_time: writes/min is the knob; a 'minute' is a unit mapping to `rate` real Db writes — NO wall-clock claim (the bench runs in seconds)
methodology.gc_grace: gcGraceMillis/graceMillis = 0 so a marked orphan sweeps the same pass — models the drain CEILING (sweep throughput vs orphan production p), not the 7-day-delayed production timing
methodology.in_band: every commit ticks runBoundedMaintenance at the profile; phasesPerTick matches each host's PRODUCTION policy — cf-free 'single' (worker.ts), node 'both' (server.ts)
methodology.scheduled: in-band disabled; one runScheduledMaintenance tick per simulated minute, alternating compact (even) / GC (odd) — the CF even/odd-minute cron pattern
methodology.tail_verdict: live_tail_entries axis: bounded = last-third mean not > 1.5x first-third mean AND grew <= one fold slice (converged sawtooth); growing = climbs past both. First-vs-last is sound here because the tail starts near steady state (no cold-bucket ramp).
methodology.objects_verdict: object_count axis: same thirds framing but (1) MID-third vs last-third — object_count ramps from 0 on a cold bucket, so the first third is unrepresentative warm-up; (2) additive floor only (grew > working_set), no 1.5x ratio gate — a slow monotonic climb on a large baseline that never plateaus is a genuine unbounded GC-drain backlog even below 1.5x, and working_set is the steady-state oscillation band below which growth is fold sawtooth noise.
methodology.verdict: combined: bounded ONLY IF both axes bounded; else growing, annotated with the offending axis — growing (tail) / growing (objects) / growing (tail+objects). This is the field a decision reads first, so it means 'nothing is growing without bound'.

invariants.cf_free_drain_ratio: gcMaxSweeps/gcInterval = 10/4 = 2.5 (>= orphan-production p keeps object count bounded — graduation.md §7.1)

note_baseline: Checked-in representative baseline. Per-minute `samples` retained for the bounded exemplar (in-band/cf-free/30 = the bounded sawtooth at the M-size envelope, bounded on BOTH the tail and objects axes) AND for EVERY cell whose combined verdict is `growing` (so the monotonic object-count climb on scheduled/cf-free and scheduled/node is visible in the checked-in file); clearly-bounded cells carry summary fields only — regenerate full trajectories with `pnpm bench:maintenance-backlog`.
